name: Maceda, Allariz, and Baños de Molgas Loop
description: Cycle the moderate 35.5-mile Castelo de Meceda - Allariz Return route, exploring historical sites and diverse landscapes in Galicia.
region_intro: Road cycling around Maceda, located in the Ourense province of Galicia, Spain, offers diverse terrain characterized by a constant succession of steep hills and mountains. The region features varied geography with paved roads suitable for speed, alongside quiet routes that traverse mountain landscapes and river valleys. Cyclists can expect significant elevation gains, with routes winding through ancient oak and chestnut forests, and past dramatic granite canyons. This area provides a compelling environment for road cyclists seeking challenging ascents and scenic rides. Best road cycling routes around Maceda The most popular road cycling route is Maceda, Allariz, and Baños de Molgas Loop, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route connects historical towns with varied terrain, offering a mix of climbs and descents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Meceda Turn, a moderate 25.0 miles (40.3 km) path. This route features a rollercoaster of steep hills and quiet roads, characteristic of the Ourense province. Local road cyclists also love the Meceda - Alto do Rodicio Return, a 14.8 miles (23.8 km) trail leading through mountain landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Road cycling around Maceda is defined by steep hills, mountain landscapes, and dramatic river canyons, with routes often passing through terraced vineyards and ancient forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Maceda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Maceda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Maceda?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Maceda, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 300 times.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Maceda?
A: Road cycling in Maceda is characterized by a constant succession of steep hills and mountains, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience. The region features varied geography with paved roads suitable for speed, alongside quiet routes that traverse mountain landscapes and river valleys. Expect significant elevation gains throughout your rides.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling around Maceda?
A: The region boasts stunning natural beauty. You'll encounter dramatic granite walls of the Sil River canyons, ancient terraced vineyards, and serene oak and chestnut forests. Many routes offer breathtaking vistas of these landscapes, especially within the nearby Ribeira Sacra.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several historical and cultural attractions enhance the cycling experience. You can cycle past the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24553204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maceda Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, partially excavated into natural rock. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4012022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa Cristina&lt;/a&gt; in the Ribeira Sacra is another significant historical site accessible via cycling routes.

Q: Which viewpoints offer the best panoramic views for cyclists in Maceda?
A: The region is dotted with numerous viewpoints offering spectacular panoramas. Notable ones include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cividade Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, both providing stunning perspectives of the Sil River Canyon and its terraced vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcones de Madrid Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; (Os Torgás Viewpoint) is also highly recommended.

Q: Are there options for different skill levels, from easy to challenging?
A: Absolutely. Maceda offers a diverse network of routes suitable for various abilities. There are 4 easy routes for beginners, 20 moderate routes for intermediate cyclists, and 18 difficult routes for experienced riders seeking a significant challenge with substantial elevation gains.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from Maceda?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in Maceda. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933963966/maceda-allariz-and-banos-de-molgas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maceda, Allariz, and Baños de Molgas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that connects historical towns with varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Maceda?
A: The mild climate for much of the year makes Maceda conducive for cycling training and tours. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Where can cyclists find parking in Maceda?
A: Maceda, as a municipality welcoming outdoor activities, typically offers public parking options within the town center. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas or consult local signage upon arrival.

Q: What kind of amenities (cafes, accommodation) are available for cyclists in Maceda?
A: Maceda and its surrounding towns provide various amenities for visitors. You'll find local cafes and restaurants to refuel, and accommodation options ranging from guesthouses to hotels, including the rehabilitated Maceda Castle which functions as a hotel.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Maceda?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24553204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Maceda – Castro Caldelas Castle loop from Maceda&lt;/a&gt; offer significant distances, covering over 130 kilometers with substantial elevation, providing an excellent challenge for long-distance enthusiasts.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Maceda?
A: The road cycling routes in Maceda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging ascents, the quiet roads, and the breathtaking views of river canyons and terraced vineyards.

faqs.question: Where does the Castelo de Meceda - Allariz Return route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is designed as a return trip, so you can conveniently start from either Castelo de Maceda or Allariz. Both locations offer parking options, making it flexible to begin your ride from the point that suits you best.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour is approximately 57.1 kilometers long. Most cyclists can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 52 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castelo de Meceda - Allariz Return route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as a moderate road ride, featuring mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for racebikes. However, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved, adding a bit more challenge. You'll experience an elevation gain and loss of around 880 meters, so expect rolling hills.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the picturesque Allariz-Maceda geodestination. You'll pass by the historic Castillo de Maceda and the Thermal Waters of Baños de Molgas. The region is also part of the Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, offering views of river plains, mixed woodlands, and unique habitats like 'Veigas' and 'Sebes'. Allariz itself boasts a historic quarter and river recognized with a European Urban and Regional Planning Award.

faqs.question: Is the Castelo de Meceda - Allariz Return route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires good fitness due to its length and elevation changes. While mostly on paved roads, some unpaved sections add to the challenge. Beginners with a good level of fitness and some road cycling experience might manage, but it's not an 'easy' ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region's natural beauty can be enjoyed across seasons. For comfortable cycling, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required for cycling this route through the Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is the Castelo de Meceda - Allariz Return route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily a road cycling tour, if you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are comfortable with being in a trailer or can keep pace safely on roads. Always keep dogs on a leash in populated areas and near wildlife, and be mindful of traffic. Check local regulations for specific areas.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), Camiño Xabrés por Verín, and Mtb Camiño Xabrés por Verín, among others like Camiño do Agro and Camiño do Outeiro.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route, particularly in Allariz or Maceda?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Allariz and Maceda are key points on the route and offer amenities. Allariz, with its historic quarter, is a good place to find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Maceda also has options for refreshments and places to stay, making either a convenient stop or starting point.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike tour, essential packing includes your helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools. Given the elevation changes, consider layers of clothing. Don't forget sunscreen and sunglasses.

waypoints.name: Thermal Waters of Baños de Molgas
